old_claim.note: Triangulation Surveying provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the node is scoped to cadastral parcel/property administration, while triangulation is a mapping method that may support later surveys without defining cadastral state programs.

source_shape.source_locator: Kain and Baigent trace cadastral maps as state property-mapping instruments and describe early modern cadastral survey for taxation in the northern Netherlands from a 1533 order.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports cadastral property mapping for state taxation rather than triangulation as a required method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Triangulation belongs to surveying technique history; keeping it direct would make one mapping method a prerequisite for all cadastral-state survey programs.

ontology_before: State Cadastral Surveys depended on Triangulation Surveying, implying triangulation defined the state cadastral program.
ontology_after: State Cadastral Surveys depends directly on cadastral maps and tax accounting; triangulation remains a neighboring surveying method.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: triangulation_surveying is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to a specific triangulated national cadastral survey.
A stronger source shows the 1533 cadastral-state anchor used triangulation as the defining method.
A separate triangulated_cadastre node is added and should carry the triangulation edge.
short_reason: Triangulation is method context, not the cadastral-state definition.
